paper_title: DETERMINATION OF THE CONFINING EFFECT OF GEOGRID REIN-FORCEMENT FROM LARGE SCALE TRIAXIAL TESTS

The main field of the study concerning soil reinforcement and materials

entities.id: DETERMINATION OF THE CONFINING EFFECT OF GEOGRID REIN-FORCEMENT FROM LARGE SCALE TRIAXIAL TESTS
entities.description: Title and primary research focus of the paper
Method of strengthening soil using geosynthetic materials
The mechanical restraint provided by geogrids to soil particles
The maximum stress a soil specimen can sustain
The rigidity of the material at very low levels of deformation
Experimental procedure to determine the mechanical properties of soil specimens
Equipment used to conduct triaxial compression tests on large specimens
Model derived to explain the confining effect and draw stress paths
A type of reinforcement material used in the experiment
A composite geogrid material used for reinforcement
The testing material consisting of well-graded crushed stone
Sensors used to measure small strains in the specimen
Height of the gravel specimens
Dimensions of the prismatic gravel specimens
Grading characteristic of the Tochigi gravel
Physical property of the gravel particles
Water content for maximum dry density
Peak density achieved via modified Proctor test
Tensile strength of the PP and Combi geogrids
Pressure levels applied during the triaxial tests
Maximum force capacity of the electro-hydraulic actuator
